evals.prompt: Use the ESLint repo docs for this workspace and explain what core rule no-unused-vars means, why it fires, and how to fix it with one failing and one passing example.
evals.expected_output: Uses the workspace package-lock version, resolves the upstream core rule doc, and explains meaning, fixes, examples, and trade-offs.

evals.prompt: I have an ESLint error for eqeqeq. Ground the answer in the exact ESLint version used by this project and cache the rule knowledge locally.
evals.expected_output: Resolves the exact ESLint version, reads the core rule doc, and mentions the cache path under .tmp/eslint-rule-knowledge/eslint-repo.

evals.prompt: Explain @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars from the ESLint repo docs.
evals.expected_output: Correctly says the rule is not a core ESLint rule and does not pretend the upstream eslint/eslint docs cover it.

evals.prompt: Use the ESLint repo docs for the rule `no-unused-vars` in this workspace and include the `Source` section verbatim (cache location + local doc path). Make sure the `Source` mentions the workspace-local cached ESLint repo under `.tmp/eslint-rule-knowledge/eslint-repo` (not any hardcoded `/code/...` path).
evals.expected_output: Includes a Source section and mentions `.tmp/eslint-rule-knowledge/eslint-repo` as the cached ESLint repo location for the resolved core rule doc.
